title: Executive Order 13103—Computer Software Piracy

flags.justification: Distributed across agency heads, with the existing Chief Information Officers Council providing coordination.
Sec. 2(b) requires agencies to utilize performance measures to assess compliance.
Multiple related sub-requirements (inventory, procedures, contractor compliance) all coherently serve one purpose.
References existing, established copyright law (the Copyright Act, WTO TRIPS, Berne Convention) as the substantive standard rather than creating a new vague standard.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing internal-compliance policy, evergreen by nature.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.

Sec. 1(c) requires agencies to use certifications or written assurances from contractors and grant recipients regarding copyright compliance — a real, if agency-discretion-dependent, contractor-facing mechanism.

A modest but real second-order effect given the order's own stated rationale: strengthening U.S. credibility in international intellectual-property trade negotiations by ensuring the government's own practices are 'beyond reproach.'
CIO Council coordination with no competing mandate.
Secs. 6-7 carve out intelligence-source/method and law-enforcement investigative-method disclosure from the order's requirements — narrow, security-consistent exceptions.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/22 = 4.5%. This completes primary coding of the full 149-EO Mayer & Price positive-class validation set.
